title: Maximum Product of Subsequences With an Alternating Sum Equal to K
performanceTarget: top 1% execution time and overall performance
note: C proposal for this challenge: the minimum-memory champion. The recommended and fastest-runtime proposal is in C++ (see crossReferences).

variants.languageReason: C gives direct allocation of exact-width uint64_t bitsets and avoids C++ container/runtime overhead, minimizing the memory footprint for the product-row DP while preserving native execution.
variants.approachJustification: Use the same compressed product-indexed DP as the C++ champion, but size each alternating-sum bitset to exactly 2 * sum(nums) + 1 bits and implement shifts manually.

reasoningSummary: The C variant keeps the same product/parity/sum DP but uses exact-width manual bitsets. It preserves the key compression facts: sum(nums) <= 1800 and products <= 5000 from factors 1..12 give at most 394 positive product rows.
